channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: HAL AERDC Issues Tender for Engine Casings, Signalling HTFE-25 & HTSE-1200 Move Toward Certification Phase
item.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) has issued a limited tender (Ref: D/DGM/OS/AE2601151003) for the supply of Air Intake Casings and Gear Box Casings under the aviation goods category, a development that points to a deeper transition within India’s indigenous aero-engine programs. While the tender appears routine on the surface, its technical context and sourcing approach indicate that HAL’s Aero Engine Research and Development Centre (AERDC) is moving from design validation into expanded hardware fabrication for endurance and certification testing.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Jammu based Machfly Aerospace Develop MF-100 ‘SonicJet’ Engine for UAVs and Missiles
item.description: An IIT Jammu-based college lab, working in collaboration with Machfly Aerospace (SAP), is developing a compact propulsion system named MF-100 “SonicJet”, aimed at powering next-generation UAVs, missiles, and UCAV platforms. The program reflects a growing push toward indigenous micro-turbine and small jet engine technologies, a segment that has traditionally seen heavy import dependence.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DRDO’s VL-SRSAM Nears Induction, Production Clearance Expected Soon
item.description: The Vertical Launch Short Range Surface-to-Air Missile (VL-SRSAM) program has entered its final pre-induction phase, marking a major milestone in the Indian Navy’s effort to modernize its shipborne air defence architecture. Developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO), the system has demonstrated consistent progress through a series of successful trials, including a key flight-test conducted on March 26, 2025, from the Integrated Test Range (ITR) at Chandipur.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF Launches Advanced Drone Interceptor Program After Operation Sindoor to Counter Emerging Aerial Threats
item.description: In the aftermath of Operation Sindoor, the Indian Air Force (IAF) is moving to rapidly strengthen its close-range counter-drone capabilities, with a new program focused on autonomous drone interception systems. The South Western Air Command, headquartered in Gandhinagar, has issued a Request for Information (RFI) to Indian industry, signaling the start of a structured effort to develop indigenous solutions capable of detecting, tracking, and neutralising hostile unmanned aerial threats.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF’s In-House MRO Strategy vs Global Outsourcing Trend: Strategic Divergence in Military Aviation Sustainment
item.description: A clear divergence is emerging in how major air forces approach maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) of advanced aviation assets. Globally, the dominant model has shifted toward industry-led sustainment frameworks, where original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) assume responsibility for availability, lifecycle support, and performance guarantees. In contrast, the Indian Air Force (IAF) is consolidating an in-house MRO ecosystem, beginning with platforms powered by the GE F404 engine, through its Base Repair Depots (BRDs).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army Turns to Civilian Hackers to Secure Military Drones Under Technology Roadmap 2026
item.description: The Indian Army is redefining how it secures its rapidly expanding drone fleet by actively involving civilian cybersecurity experts to stress-test critical communication systems. This initiative, embedded within the Army Technology Roadmap 2026, marks a decisive shift from viewing drones as mere aerial platforms to treating them as fully networked “ICT systems on wings.” In doing so, the Army is aligning itself with modern warfare realities, where the battle for electromagnetic dominance and data integrity often determines operational success as much as kinetic capability.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: “Pakistan Trap” Explained: Why U.S. Strategic Choices Still Tilt Toward Islamabad Over New Delhi
item.description: A provocative opinion piece in EurAsian Times by Israeli analyst Shay Gal argues that Washington’s long-standing preference for Pakistan over India is not rooted in values, democracy, or institutional strength—but in cold, operational utility. The thesis challenges a common Indian assumption that alignment with the U.S. should naturally deepen based on shared democratic credentials, instead framing American choices as historically transactional and interest-driven.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: NTPC exploring two nuclear power units of 1,400 MW in Bihar’s Banka
item.description: As part of its energy diversification plan, state-owned NTPC is looking to set up two nuclear units of 700 MW each in the Banka district of Bihar, which could entail an investment of around Rs 25,000 crore. At present, NTPC officials are conducting a feasibility study for two 700-MW nuclear units in the district, located around 250 km from Patna, a government official said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Jyotiraditya Scindia Announces Rs 2,500cr Defence Plant, Unveils Infra Projects In Kolaras
item.description: Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia on Saturday announced a major industrial boost for Kolaras town in Madhya Pradesh, unveiling plans for a Rs 2,500 crore defence manufacturing plant along with a series of infrastructure projects aimed at accelerating the region’s development.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Outcomes That Endure: Army’s Strong Message Ahead Of Op Sindoor Anniversary
item.description: Days ahead of the anniversary of Operation Sindoor, the Indian Army on Sunday shared a social media post highlighting the precision strikes that destroyed nine terror camps linked to outfits such as Lashkar-e-Taiba and Jaish-e-Mohammed in Pakistan and Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: PM Modi Calls Kalpakkam Fast Breeder Reactor Criticality ‘Historic Milestone’ In Nuclear Journey
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Sunday hailed India’s indigenously designed Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor at Kalpakkam, which recently achieved criticality, marking a major advance in the second stage of the country’s three-stage civil nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Relieved to learn that President Trump, First Lady and Vice President are safe’: PM Modi
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Sunday said he was relieved that US President Donald Trump, First Lady Melania Trump and Vice President JD Vance were safe and unharmed after a security incident at the White House Correspondents’ dinner in Washington.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: INS Sunayana Heading To Singapore Under IOS Sagar Mission
item.description: Indian Naval Ship (INS) Sunayana is heading to Singapore under the IOS Sagar mission with the theme of ‘One Ocean, One mission’, the Indian High Commission in Singapore stated on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DIG calls for heightened vigilance, robust security grid along IB in J-K’s Kathua
item.description: Strategic sensitivity of border areas calls for strict vigilance and heightened alertness along the International Border in Jammu and Kashmir’s Kathua district, Deputy Inspector General of Police, Jammu-Samba-Kathua Range Shridhar Patil has said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 47 Maoists From Chhattisgarh Surrender Before Telangana DGP
item.description: As many as 47 underground cadres of CPI (Maoist) from Chhattisgarh, including Dandakaranya Special Zonal Committee (DKSZC) member and in-charge of South Bastar Divisional Committee Hemla Iythu alias Vijja, surrendered before the Telangana Director General of Police B. Shivdhar Reddy here on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: admin
